dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: First I thought you stumbled back here by mistake. But you just keep on showing up. Something you want to tell me, babe?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, I... was just drawn here to you. By your trail of rubbish! You're luring me, aren't you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey, thank you, Eris! You didn't throw your rubbish toward me as I approached... that couldn't have been easy.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: It was easy, Trouble, because I forgot! I've had a lot on my mind, OK? So you don't have to give me such a hard time as soon as I slip up on even the slightest thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wasn't giving you a hard time, I was genuinely pleased, but also completely mistaken apparently. Do you really expect me to keep picking up after you forever?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Well it's not like Moros is gonna do it! Besides, how else could I lure you to where I have you all to myself?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I knew you were trying to lure me!

endLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
endLines.text: And you still take the bait!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Augh what gives? The Adamant Rail's back to normal! I must have hit a hidden switch on it or something, but... it doesn't even work here, let alone transform!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Take the hint, Eris. You may be the bearer of the Rail for the time being, but it doesn't answer to you alone. Even if it lets you aim and shoot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: If only it let me aim and shoot right now! You better not be behind this, Trouble, or if you are... you change it back! I liked it more the other way!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: It can hear you, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, could you do me a favor and ask your woodsy pal over there to keep it down? I practically can't hear myself think!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps it's because no thoughts of any note have been going through your head. You really expect me to ask Artemis not to sing on your behalf? Do it yourself!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Fine, maybe I will! Or maybe I'll keep sulking and complaining about it! I haven't decided yet.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Take all the time you need.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Well if it isn't Charon over there! I heard all the heavy breathing, and sure enough. Got him eating out of the palm of your hand like all the other animals around here, huh.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your utter disrespect to one of the most dedicated servants of the realm and your own brother is duly noted, Eris. But if it matters, Charon is here of his own volition.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, kind of like how you're here of your own volition too? Taken as a baby, not allowed to see even a glimpse of the outside world till you've been trained as an assassin. Your choice!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: No one has forced me to do this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: So what's been going on with you and Chronos anyway? You keep beating him but he keeps coming back! I like how pointless it seems, but... there has to be more to it, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Eris. I'm not about to reveal the critical details of my task, especially to someone who had every opportunity to be part of it to begin with. Go about your business.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: How about you just give me a little hint. Are you planning to use his own powers against him? Break free our family members one by one? Anything like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I really thought you had me that time, babe, but... I guess not. Maybe later. You must be mad you can't get back at me right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wouldn't break the peace here even if I could, Eris. But you really test my self-control. When next I see you at the edge of the Rift, I'll not hold back.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
endLines.text: Ooh, she's angry! Just the way I like it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You know back when I was just getting started, I didn't appreciate you placing some wretched curse on me. I might have settled things with Chronos even sooner!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: And why would I have wanted that? Also thanks for reminding me, I almost forgot I could just curse you every night. That might work even better than the Rail!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If you so much as dare, I shall pluck every feather from your wings, one by one by one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've seen firsthand the suffering out there, from all the endless warring between Chronos and the gods. But you're entirely unmoved?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Who says I'm unmoved? On the contrary, it makes me all emotional! I'm trying to live in the moment and everything, but... I just know I'm going to look back on this and think, these were the best of times.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: For you; not for anybody else.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Eris, you... you said before that I don't even know what I'm missing. What did you mean by that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Didn't mean your family, that's for sure! Come on, look at yourself. You've never lived a day in your life! That heart beating in your chest? You think it's only there to pump your blood! Just another muscle!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The heart is a muscle. What, should I be out feasting? When Olympus is in peril, and the Underworld in utter disarray?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Maybe! But you just want everything nice, and orderly, and predictable, and boring.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You don't even speak with Headmistress anymore, do you? Makes her relationship with Nemesis look positively healthy by comparison.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: She said a lot of nasty things about me, babe. And not just to my face. All because I didn't fit her model of a star disciple! I had to move on. It's just, I don't need anybody like that in my life.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Eris, you repeatedly stole from her, kept using the cauldron to create explosives, and my tent for target practice, vandalized Hypnos in his slumber, salted the garden, and continually harassed Shades about the way they died...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, Miss Perfect, so I made a few slip-ups! Doesn't make it OK for Hecate to lecture me, in private, where I couldn't even get full credit for the things I did. You don't know the half of it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: That Heracles sure is something, isn't he? Too bad there's not much going on between his ears. That lion pelt he likes to strut around in probably has got more smarts than him.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What makes you say that? Other than your general tendency to speak ill of others. Heracles seems no fool to me; a bit disillusioned, maybe.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: His club does all the thinking for him, and most of the talking, too. Ehh, you're both gullible! Maybe that's why you think he's so great. Or, maybe he's just your type?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Aw, Hypnos is gone? I had big plans for him involving a bag of cats and a couple bottles of Nectar you gave me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then I saved him from a terrible fate of some sort. Perhaps at some point down the line he'll thank me properly, as he was in a bit of a hurry to depart.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Why don't you let me kill you next time we're out there on the Rift, and chances are, you'll wash on down into the River Styx, and wind up right in front of him again? He's the greeter at the House of Hades isn't he?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He's not a greeter. He holds a position of high honor.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
endLines.text: Welcome to the House of Hades! He's a greeter, babe.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Why are you really here, Eris? Tell me Chronos didn't put you up to this. You've made quite clear that you don't want me to succeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Babe, I'm almost hurt! You don't get me at all, do you? Just because I'm not rooting for you doesn't mean I'm rooting for the other guy.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then who exactly are you rooting for, pray tell?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
endLines.text: I'll let you puzzle that one out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: If I have to listen to another one of your conversations with Moros, I am going to vomit. Though, you'll just clean that up too, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're not to eavesdrop on my private conversations. Particularly if they induce in you a sense of nausea! You might do well to speak with your brother yourself, sometime.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
endLines.text: I'd much rather see how long he'll keep pretending I'm not here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've been back a while yet still isolate yourself. Your brother makes more of an effort to fit in, but he struggles too... you may have more in common than you think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh really? Maybe I'll go over and tell him you said that! What, you think just because most of the world hates his guts same as mine that we're alike?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, I'd not put it that way at all... though I do think each of you has been misunderstood in your own right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: What's not to understand? I'm Strife; he's Doom. These are simple concepts, babe! Though granted, most of the world is pretty stupid...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You're really getting under Nemmie's skin lately! Saw her run out in a huff, clanky clank, not long after talking to you. What'd you say that got her so worked up? I'll have to add it to my arsenal.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wouldn't know. We've discussed my task from time to time, and she's interested in helping out however she can. I think she's just very determined to see our plans succeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, I hear you! Fine. A witch never reveals her tricks, something like that? Well, just watch yourself with Sis, is all I'll say. She won't let anybody get too far ahead!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I can't believe you stood me up last night, Trouble! Do you know how demeaning it is having to wait around for you at a beach, in the middle of the night, all alone?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What? We had no plans to meet as I recall, else I'd have honored them. You truly expect me to abandon the main focus of my task, just to fight with you again and again?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought I felt something was all... that our times at the beach might be just a little more special to you than your boring job. But I guess I was wrong...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Not seen you topside in a while, babe. I didn't want to say anything but it's starting to feel a little personal, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Eris, have you considered the possibility that when I pursue my task within the Underworld, you could not be further from my mind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: That's what they all say. You know what I think? You're afraid. Not just to face me, but to face your feelings. Fine! Go run away into the Underworld some more.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Why do I speak to you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've such disdain for what we're trying to achieve. You act as though nothing was lost. Don't you want to see Nyx again? Or Thanatos?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't I want to see my mother again, or brother number-I-have-no-idea? Pff, hahaha, no. They were even more stuck up than you. No offense.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...How can you say such things about your relatives? Were they crueler to you than this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx always wanted things her way. Why should she, or anybody, have that kind of power over me? Because she made me? She didn't ask my permission. And I don't need hers.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: So what'd you think of Nyx? You met her, right? Now you know where Nemmie gets her looks, and where I get my personality.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What? I hardly got to know her but even still, Nyx didn't seem anything like you at all. She was very elegant.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Aw, thanks, babe. But beneath that cold exterior she's got a wild side, I swear. Why else would she have so many kids all by herself? She's out of her mind!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Look... you're right I've never known what it was like before Chronos returned. You keep insinuating it was worse than now. How so?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Already told you, babe! Your father, my mother, whole inner circle... they got off bossing everyone around! Didn't give me space to spread my wings. Bunch of Olympus wannabes, but with mood lighting!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...So you prefer living in a state of total disorder and constant mayhem to living under a principled set of rules?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
endLines.text: That's a really good way to put it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You really want to know the way things used to be, Trouble? Think about everything that gets you to come out of your tent night after night. Gets you fired up! What comes to mind?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sure. Those I care about are counting on me. And, I wish to learn; to grow strong in body, mind, and spirit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Inspiring. Now, imagine having everything you want... so you're left wanting nothing at all....

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, I can't imagine that. So, everyone besides you was self-indulgent and unmotivated? But now the roles are reversed, I suppose?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't get you people, honestly. So set on making things the way they used to be. You, especially! You don't even know what you're missing!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We're trying to set things right, not make them how they used to be. Though, what's your point, Eris?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: What's my point? What's your point? All of you are so obsessed with there being a Point to everything. You do know there isn't one, don't you? Can't just let loose and have a good time?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Not under the present circumstances, no.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Eris, what's the matter? Are you... have you been weeping?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: No! And... what if I was, huh? It's not like you care about me! Not you or anybody in this whole damn place.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh stop it. If you made even the slightest effort not to be a scourge on these Crossroads, perhaps you'd have more companions. I keep foolishly believing that you'll change.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You're not my companion. No one is! No one I'm close to... no one I can depend on... share my secrets with...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Look... I'll not betray your trust if you need someone to confide in. Provided what you share won't put the others whom I care about in any sort of peril.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch... others whom you care about. You lump me in with them? Come on, babe, all we do is fight! Which I don't mind, of course! But there's much more to a relationship.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've told you to speak plain with me before. What sort of relationship are you expecting us to have when all you do is interfere with me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You're so stupid sometimes! Look, I can't help the way I feel, and I'm not exactly a good judge of character. Whatever's wrong with me, I know it can't be fixed.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: How could you possibly know that?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: When last we spoke you said whatever's wrong with you couldn't be fixed. What makes you say such things?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Appreciate all the concern but I just had a little moment that you're making a big deal. We're all a little broken, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps so. But we can better ourselves through effort and discipline, as much as we can muster. Or, we could endlessly get in each other's way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: The thing is, I don't want to have to try. Not with this, not with you. I want things to be easy, you want things to be hard. But at least we really get each other now!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey about the other night, are we... I mean, are you all right with everything? I don't know what I'm trying to say...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Babe, stop. You said you never had any regrets about me. Probably lying to yourself, but still. We had our fun, so what?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps I'm just not yet accustomed to what you consider fun. I've had a lot of other things to think about.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: And you always will! But if you don't leave room for any mayhem in your life, it's gonna be a real drag. I swear.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Mayhem? We were talking about fun.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey about the other night, are we... I mean, are you all right with everything? I don't know what I'm trying to say...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Babe, stop. As much as I like messing with your head, I can't stand it when you act all pathetic. What, you're worried you hurt my little feelings, or just having second thoughts?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Mostly the former. If I've somehow earned some of your trust, it's not my wish to ruin that, in spite of everything.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Then all you have to do is shut up about it! It's only a big deal if you decide to make it one, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You're really turning heads around here, babe. I've seen the way Nemmie and Moros look at you! What do you think, maybe it's time we finally told them about us?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh cut it out, Eris. No serious commitments, right? You happen to come from an attractive family, and we have all the time in the world. Especially once all of this is over.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I mean, I guess! Assuming this will be over at some point, or something worse doesn't come up! Anyway just messing with your head. I like how gullible you are...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Got me fair and square again, Trouble. More than a couple times! Starting to think maybe I'm losing it, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you poor thing. Please, let me cheer you up, so you'll proceed to fight against me even harder than before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You mock me when I'm vulnerable, fine. But don't expect to cry on this shoulder when things take a turn for the worse, when it comes to your stuff. Outta my face!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Babe, I can't believe you got us something for our special spot! That's so thoughtful! You really shouldn't have.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're right, I probably shouldn't have. You'll find some way to hold this over me at some point, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Guess you'll find out! And don't worry, if anyone asks, I'll just tell them it's our little secret.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I know what it's like, being pushed away. Having everybody smile to your face then stab you in the back. Well, for what it's worth... I don't believe a word they're saying about you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I see. And to whom exactly are you referring, and can you cite an example of the words purportedly said?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it's just... Odysseus and Hecate. I can't believe they think you're gonna fail, that you don't have what it takes...! I mean, I don't want you to succeed, but... I never pretended otherwise!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Great job last night, babe! Is that what you want to hear? Well tough, because you won't be hearing it from me. Other than that one time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: As much as I probably would appreciate the occasional bit of encouragement from you now that you mention it, Eris, I know it's not your tendency. I was just checking up on you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Aw, thanks! Whenever you accomplish something important it's like you're putting a knife right in my heart. Though, good to know you'll come around and check up on my corpse!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You really put up warning signs about me, babe? That seems a little immature, even for you. What if I did something like that to you, how would you like it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, I... wouldn't like it, probably, Eris. I'm sorry. I merely thought the signs would help me know when I can visit with you here... I'd not considered it was something cruel.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: It is cruel! I was so impressed! But now you're groveling about it so I guess I gave you too much credit for that one. You're not gonna take them down, are you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...It sounds like you don't want me to, so... perhaps I shall.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You really put away the signs with my face on 'em! You have a mean streak after all! Babe! I'm so proud of you!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What? Do you want your warning signs posted, or don't you? Either way I'm going to do the exact opposite, because that's what you would do to me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh you're so fired up right now! Do I want those signs up or down? You'll never know, because I swear I'll never mention 'em again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Eris, how come you're always back here on your own? You never struck me as particularly reticent, and seem to enjoy interacting with others, in your own way....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, babe, it's a bit of a weird explanation, and you're not going to like it if I tell you. Which makes me really want to tell you! But then I think about how you must really want to know, which makes me not want to tell you anymore.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm fairly certain it's not that you're just a figment of my imagination. Little voice in my head that keeps leading me astray.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
endLines.text: Long as you're not completely certain!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You don't know how good you've got it, Trouble. I grew up surrounded by siblings all thinking they were better than me. There's hoity-toity Retribution, over there! And Death, and Doom! Nobody wanted anything to do with Strife.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Moros could relate more than you know. You children of Nyx are widely feared, but so what? You don't need a lot of friends. You just need a few good ones. And the very meaning of Strife is for you to define, isn't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't hate me, babe, OK? It's not my fault I keep lying to you, manipulating you, shooting you with the Rail, making you clean up after me, saying awful things behind your back...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: While I cannot shake the sense that this is a personal failing of mine, in spite of everything, I don't hate you, Eris. Not entirely. Although... whose fault is all of that, if not your own?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: My mother's! It's Nyx's fault, that's whose! Because she literally made me what I am, and what I'll always be. I could have been Old Age, or Friendship! But, no! And I can't change my nature. No matter how hard I try.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And, approximately how hard would you say you've tried?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I mean, I haven't really, yet! But I'm going to this time, I swear!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I heard you went up top! Got to breathe some fresh surface air. What made you go and do a thing like that? Thought you were single-mindedly going after Chronos!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Figured that I might do well with a change of scenery for a bit. Though, I'm surprised you're not up there yourself, considering how things are.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: It does sound pretty exciting! But, I'd probably just be getting in the way. From what I hear, Ares has things well in hand up there. Imagine being the god of war right now. You'd be thrilled!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Your whole family would be in peril.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I figured it out! All these visits topside. You're trying to get to Olympus! Come to the family's rescue! Put an end to the war up there, is that it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I just go up there to gather fresh reagents, really. Moss, bits of bronze. Comes in handy! The Olympians are very good at taking care of themselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure they are. And they're too proud to ask for help. Just don't go ruining it for Ares when you're up there picking Moss. He's on a real hot streak lately!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sounds like you and the god of war are still pretty close. Been very busy lately, hasn't he?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: They're saying you beat Typhon single-handedly last night. One step closer to putting everything back to normal, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I did not beat Typhon single-handedly, but we managed to defeat him, yes. Though I don't think you need to fear a return to normalcy anytime soon...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I know you're trying to reassure me, but I can't help the way I feel! I ought to be enjoying all this while it lasts! The future might be boring as dirt, but right now? Things are OK.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You know how come I'm really back, don't you, Trouble? Because things finally started getting interesting. But you're trying to put a stop to it...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I won't let you cast doubt on my task. Surely you've better uses of your energy than this, and throwing all your rubbish everywhere?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: What would you even know? I have every right to be here, and you're never getting rid of me! And you're never getting rid of Chronos, either. No going back to how things were!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What compels you to fill this place with your rubbish, Eris? Do you wish to be welcome here only as a technicality?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Um, since when am I supposed to care what anybody thinks? You're all about borrowing from the earth. I'm just giving something back!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What is this stuff you're always littering about here, anyway? Cyclops Jerky? Did you happen on a limitless supply?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Know what, as a matter of fact, I did! And you'll never guess where from. This stuff is so addictive once you get into it! Want to try some?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You don't need to pick up after me, you know. I was gonna get around to it! What do you do with all that, anyway?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I present it to the Wretched Broker, through whom anything we no longer require can return to the Earth. You could do so yourself...! The Broker's right there...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, that's all right. I wouldn't want to impose! Though maybe I'll go through your things next time you're out, and have the Broker there return them to the Earth!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Eris, I have a favor to ask, although it's more of a plea: Could you please, please stop leaving your rubbish on the ground? You could just put it in the basket right nearby!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I don't know, babe! I distinctly remember when I asked you to set me up with Ares and that didn't go my way. So why should I do anything for you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't think asking me to get the god of war's attention is equivalent to simply picking up after yourself when you're essentially a guest. But if you want to be spiteful, fine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Know what your problem is, Trouble? You're too green. Not done enough hard living to tease apart the truth from whatever it is you're told.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for the feedback, Eris. You've given me a lot to think about. I regret our living here in this embattled camp just isn't hard enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: <Gasp> Sarcasm! Good, I like it. You've got a mean streak in you, I just know it, babe. And I wouldn't want you keeping it all to yourself! Share your gift with the world!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Sounds like there was a big commotion on Olympus last night! And I wasn't even around, thanks to you. How'd it go?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Just great! Almost got eaten by the Father of All Monsters trying to distract him from destroying everything in sight! You really had to be there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, babe, when somebody misses out on a good time, you ought to play it down, like, oh it wasn't anything that great. Or they'll feel bad they couldn't make it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm warning you, you better cut this out, Trouble. Messing with Chronos, messing with Typhon, messing everything up! That's supposed to be my job!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You clearly did your job already. I'm in the process of undoing it. Much like how I often pick up after you here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Well you're not gonna. And even if you do, you'll look back on this and wish things were as interesting as they are now!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wouldn't mind if things were boring for a change.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You're so worked up about this war, like it's some huge thing. Olympus loves a good war! Have 'em all the time up there, got a whole god dedicated to 'em and everything. Know how easy it is to get 'em to start fighting?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, Eris, please, enlighten me. Just how easy is it to get my relatives to descend into acts of unconscionable violence and destruction?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Well this one time, they all flew into a tizzy over a single Golden Apple. A mysterious offering, marked for the fairest one of all! They had a little dispute over who the secret admirer meant, one thing led to another, and boom!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That sounds like a simple misunderstanding, if not a prank. But you say it grew into a full-scale war? What happened then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Speaking as someone with no small experience starting wars, I gotta say... just 'cause I'm not working for Chronos, doesn't mean I don't respect what he's been able to achieve.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Eris... that was one of the worst sentences I think I've ever heard.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Go look at what he's done, objectively, and tell me I'm wrong!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoa, haha! You startled me there, Trouble! Sneaking up on me like that, I almost blasted you. You gotta be more careful!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't appreciate you brandishing that thing about. Weapons remain prohibited outside the training grounds, in case you didn't know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Weapons? Ah-ha! Hello, this is the Adamant Rail you're talking about. This baby once took Titans down! It's a work of art. And no one's taking it from me!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You ever going to tell me how you got your hands on it to begin with? It was intended to be in my father's care... yet, somehow it's in yours.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
endLines.text: Turns out he wasn't so good at protecting his own!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Please don't make me regret this, Eris, but... would you like to join me at the hot springs for a bit? Helps ease the tension.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, the springs are purified again? I've been out of the loop! Surprised I'd get another invite after last time, but... OK!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Ungh, so this is what you still do to relax? Hot as magma in this thing! Can we get out yet?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Permission denied. You'll quickly grow accustomed to the heat. Just try to concentrate on any tensions that you feel, and let the waters take them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Tensions, me? Babe. I don't need to boil alive in order to cut loose. Though good to know you've found a way to bottle tensions up. What happens if you drink this stuff?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: These springs are pure thanks partly to the Salts we use. Though even still, I wouldn't recommend imbibing them. Next thing you know, you might be purified as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I'm not worried about that! This is the way I am down to my very core. And if you don't like it, and brought me in here hoping a nice hot bath was all it'd take for me to change, well then... I hate to disappoint.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Looks like the waters haven't rid you of your tensions yet...! Sometimes a hot spring is just a hot spring, Eris. Not everything is an assault on your ideals.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: With all of you, I never can be sure. Though, this is nicer than the last time, and the heat isn't so bad right now, I guess...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh... think I drank way too much bath water back there. Is this what being purified feels like? Whew!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey how about another visit to the springs? You seemed to enjoy yourself last time, and I find it truly helps.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Well as long as it helps! Look, I don't get my wings wet for just anybody, babe, but you're special. So let's go?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...What is it like having so many siblings? I have but one and didn't even know him growing up. Though I often tried confiding in him anyway, like an imaginary friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: The more brothers and sisters you have, the bigger the disappointment you can be! And it is so embarrassing when you forget their names. Just look how us kids of Nyx get on. The only family more messed up than mine is yours!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You mean the Olympians. How do you figure? They have their differences to be sure. But they still work together to maintain the natural order of the realms.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Work together? Come on, they can barely stand each other, from the very top on down! They never should have gone into business. Always caught between their blood commitments and their jobs!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I won't deny that family adds a complicated layer to their responsibilities. But it's also a source of solidarity and strength. Look at them now, standing together against a common foe. This is the Olympus mortals love and fear.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Sorry, not impressed. They say family is a bond that can never be broken, but... as is so often the case, they're wrong. Just because someone's related to you doesn't make them special or deserving of respect.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What does it take to earn respect from you, Eris?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I actually don't even know the answer to that right now. Though, sitting naked with me in half-boiling water helps!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I had a blast there, babe. And look how clean my wings are now! Won't stay like this for long, but... still.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was thinking, why don't we break from all our pointless fighting and stop by the springs again. What do you say?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure! I like listening to whatever pops into your head while staring at you till you get all self-conscious. Let's go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm not your friend, you know. However close you think we are now, I'm just setting you up so I can really let you down.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If that were so, why would you bother warning me about it? You'd simply do it, rather than try to push me away. Well I'm not going anywhere. Especially not without my attire.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll regret it later. Everybody always does. I can make things so much worse! And the more time you give me, the more likely it'll happen. You think I'm bluffing?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, I think you probably mean it. But as for me, I don't ever want to get too comfortable. That's when I feel the most uneasy. And with you around, I doubt I ever will.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: That's probably the nicest thing that anybody's ever said to me... but it changes nothing, and nothing ever does. Hey if you don't want to get too comfortable, then what are you doing here?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Recovering from the last time you used me for target practice with the Adamant Rail. I'm used to certain trade-offs when it comes to being around you, Eris.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Just don't get too used to them, right, Trouble? But at least you seem to understand there's no getting rid of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Well! You look like you're all set for some more target practice after that. You know where to find me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey... what would you say about a visit to the hot springs, to cleanse yourself of all your foul deeds? And me of mine?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, haha, well... you know, I'd feel way too self-conscious there with anybody else. Sorry!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's fine if you can't make it though would you be up for visiting the springs with me? Could help to calm us down.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Last thing I want, babe, is to calm down. Besides, the springs are all the way over there, and I'm already minding my own business here! As you should mind yours.

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: A visit to the springs, just you and me? Aw, babe!

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: Of everybody in your little Crossroads, you'd choose me? I'm honored, babe!

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: I've gotten real dirty since we last visited the springs, so I don't really see why not!

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...This is nice...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Could stay here all night...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Let our worries melt away...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...I needed this...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Whew, it's been a time...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Warmer than usual tonight...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...So peaceful here...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Just what I needed...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Whew, this is good...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Always pleasant...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...<Sigh> It's nice here...

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: ...No matter how much of this stuff I drink, it never seems to clean me on the inside!

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: ...Things are pretty awkward with us, huh? Though if it means more visits to the springs, what do I care?

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: ...Just keep your hands where I can see 'em, babe. Don't want you getting too comfortable!

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: Whew! I'm so relaxed it's almost like I wouldn't want to kill you anymore!

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: Felt good, didn't it? The only reason to do anything, Trouble!

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: Mm! Next time I might not let you drag me out of there so soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Ungh, I'm still worked up after last night. When did you get so strong? I don't stand a chance against you anymore. Hey, want to go someplace with me, blow off some steam?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not entirely certain I do. At the taverna at least there are plenty of witnesses in case you try anything untoward.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: C'mon, what are you afraid of? Not like you can't take me in a fight! Anyway, it's not too far. I swear.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Wait this is it? You weren't jesting when you said it wasn't far. What's so special about this spot?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I like it here is all. No frogs croaking, no Shades wandering around... no one to catch us if we fool around a little bit...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: By fool around a little bit you mean... oh, right. Eris, look, I'm not prepared for any serious commitment here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Babe, I've never made a serious commitment in my life. I just like to have a little fun from time to time after a hard night trying to shoot you to pieces! What do you say?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Choice_ErisBecomingCloser01

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...While I should probably regret a great many things about our relationship, the truth is, I never have. And besides... I like to learn from my mistakes.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well! I think I learned a lot this evening, all in all. Though I should probably get back. If anybody asks, we were at the fishing pier.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: We are at the fishing pier, babe. And what does it even matter if anybody asks? Who cares? We were just having a good time! That's not forbidden here, is it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It isn't, no. So I suppose you now go back to trying to shoot me to pieces night after night like nothing happened?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You got it! What, did you really think I was gonna fall so hard that I'd give up on everything that makes me me? Please. No serious commitments, remember?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I do. And I don't mean to think like mortals here, it's just... I don't understand you sometimes, Eris. To be so... carefree.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll let you in on a little secret, babe: We like what we don't understand or can't have. Anyway that's always my excuse! See you around!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It means a lot that you would ask, because I know it's not been easy to get close to anybody here. But this isn't something that I'm looking for right now. Whatever it is we have between us... I've come to like it just the way it is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: That's how I feel! And I get it, long as you're doing what you want instead of what everybody else wants. Though, mind if I tell everybody that we fooled around here anyway?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You seem as though you're looking for Trouble, Eris.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Been a little while since we blew off steam, hasn't it, babe?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: See, I'm not always bad. Sometimes I'm horrible.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Whew. No arguments.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What's on your mind there, Strife?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Want to get out of here? And by that I mean go over there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Always a pleasure messing with you, babe!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Likewise, Eris.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You want something, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I want to disappear for a bit, and you're coming with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, Trouble, you're free to go. Get out of here, will you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...All right, all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: May I help you with something this eve?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you say we check if the old fishing pier's all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Inspection complete! Want to go report our latest findings?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Let's keep them to ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh hi again there babe. Can you guess what I'm thinking?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hi again there, Eris. And yes, I believe I can.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Well! You're definitely getting pretty good at reading minds.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...You know, I try.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Something you wish to discuss with me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Whatever it is you need, babe, I'm here for you. Sometimes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: ...And that takes care of that I think, at least for now!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I concur.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Think you might have missed a spot back there, Trouble?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Just keep at it, whatever it is you think you've got to prove!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: What's the big rush? We've got all the time in the world!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You know this whole place is turning into a real dump...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Real shame what's been happening between us lately, Trouble.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: See Nemmie around, tell her I really wanted to talk to her, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Life sure moves fast, even for deathless goddesses, huh, Trouble?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh, don't give me that look. I belong here just as much as you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You're not getting anywhere, babe. None of us are!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: How about you mind your own business, Trouble, and lemme mind mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Uh-oh, she's here! I'd better fly away and hide!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Thought I'd drop in again and check up on everything, y'know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You must be really making a difference out there, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Whew, it's all just been a little much lately, y'know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Move along there, Trouble, I don't really want to talk to you right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, babe! You know rumors gonna fly if they catch us like this!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh, looks like you just woke up! Couldn't wait to see me again, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Got my eye on you, Trouble. So you better keep your nose clean.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: All this stress, it isn't getting to you, is it, babe?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Why talk to me? Do what you want! Nobody cares!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I just don't get what's wrong with liking it when everything gets totally messed up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You better not fix everything anytime soon, you hear me, Trouble?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Whew, you really worked me over out there on the Rift last night, babe!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You must be keeping count of every single time you beat me, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Come by the Rift again sometime! It's not like I can shoot you here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Race you to the Rift, babe! Last one over there's the first one to get shot!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, I'm sort of busy doing other things right now, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Move along now, Trouble!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's all just stand around!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: D'you miss me, Trouble? Thought I'd flit on over to your neck of the woods again, since I'm still welcome here and safe and everything. What you been up to lately?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You know, not much really, Eris! So, what brings you back? For a while there, I thought maybe you really were gone for good this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Hate to disappoint, but I just couldn't stay away! Especially not with everything we're in the middle of right now. You have been real busy lately, huh?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Whatever it is you heard, please keep it confidential.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Miss me, Trouble? Thought I'd flit on over for a bit again, and see how everybody's doing, since I'm welcome here and safe still, aren't I? Though it has been a little while.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's not been long enough. Much as I wished you'd grown past your childishness, Eris, I can't imagine you've returned for any reason other than to keep it up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: What, you can't imagine I just want to visit my big sister, if not you? I get it, you're still mad I slowed you down before, but... I will not do that again, I swear. So truce?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How about we head down to the fishing pier a bit? Not like it's far out of the way or anything, and it's a change of pace.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure, I'm easy, babe! Except when I'm not, but right now I could go for anything! Even stand around for fish to bite!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Know what I hate more than just about anything else? Having to be perfectly quiet while standing perfectly still. What have I done to deserve this torture?!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know it's difficult, which is part of the reason to do it from time to time... just concentrate on being who you are and being here. A fishing pier isn't strictly required.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Being focused in on the moment, come on! How can you not always be doing that? At least in other moments I'd be doing what I want. Not caving to your every demand!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If you don't find anything worthwhile in this, you don't have to do it again. But since we're here, why not find out if we can catch something?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: All you are gonna catch is my fist in your face if you keep on mouthing off to me, babe! Though wait, shut up! Think I just saw something swim by!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Babe I had a totally amazing time, I swear! And, you don't stink at all!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The fishing pier's wide open as you can see... any impulse to make use of it a while?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Mmm, a trip to the fishing-hole involves a lot more being still and quiet than I think I can handle right now, thanks.

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: Oh no, not this again. But then again, who cares? Let's go!

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: Hm! Think I could probably disturb the waters just enough to get the fishies biting, though there's one way to find out!

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: I'd much rather mess with you than a bunch of mindless fish, but I could probably do both at the same time!

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: That's more than enough being still and quiet for one night.

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: We'll never be the same again, I hope you know, Trouble.

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: That's enough concentrating on being here for one night!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You traitor! How dare you stand against me and our cause? And, of all the nerve to show your face again here! You'll be cast out for what you've done!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Sorry, babe, but I'm not going anywhere. Thank Nyx and Hecate and their unbreakable oaths! Besides, you got me all wrong.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh? Perhaps I merely mistook you for one of Nyx's other daughters who readily shot at me using the Rail of Adamant!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: No, that was me all right, and I had a great time! But I take umbrage with the traitor thing. Think I made clear I wasn't on your side.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Hoo, babe, you know what? You put up more of a fight out there than I figured! The Rail was so hot to the touch, I could hardly stand it!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Pleased to be of entertainment for your benefit. Is that all this is to you, then? You jeopardize our continued existence, just for a cheap thrill?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't go acting like there wasn't part of you that felt a little thrill out there, yourself. And now that you've had a taste, you want more, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I want you out of these Crossroads, and out of my blasted path.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
endLines.text: Eh, too bad we can't have it all...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Is this what you want, Eris? To have our aimless clashes, only then to sneer at one another here, again and again? Such a worthy cause.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Who are you to say what makes a worthy cause? 'Cause, yeah! I happen to be quite all right with everything! It's genuinely been a real blast!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Look, I... do want you to feel welcome here and safe. It's not just something that we say. May this gift of Nectar show my words are true.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Wow... made with real Golden Apples! Thanks, babe. You're the only one who's made a real effort to bring me into the fold! Say, why don't you take this? It isn't much, but I've been holding onto it, in case I found a friend...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: But, you already got me one of these. Don't you have anything else? Mmhmhmhm, only messing with you, babe! I'll get you back for this sometime, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Please don't... I mean, I just wanted you to have it since I know you have a taste for this stuff, but it's been harder to come by. Anyway...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Babe, no one ever gets me anything like this! Except the last couple of times you got me the exact same thing. But oh, I feel so bad I don't have anything for you!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's only a gift, to make you feel a little better, not worse! Don't mention it, just... you enjoy. But, please don't leave the empty bottle on the ground.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Just had to see my reaction to another one of these, huh? You say it's only a gift, but you're probably keeping meticulous track of every Nectar you dole out. Hoping whoever gets one's gonna like you just a little more each time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Congratulations on making this weird, Eris... Fine, you figured me out! I'm desperate for your approval and affection. Yours and everyone's! Is that what you think?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You said it, babe, not me. Now, come off it. Though, if you can keep a secret...? I do like you just a little more, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You know I've tried brewing Nectar myself. Got most of the key ingredients, except my batches never tasted right! Not like the stuff you get your hands on.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nectar's always best when it's a gift. Ever had somebody else give yours a try? Perhaps it's better than you think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, no, because... for that I'd need a friend. Though I could always trick somebody into drinking it...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Right when you trotted over to me here, I wished you'd bring another Nectar, since I'm out. And, lo and behold! It's like you're a mind-reader... or a dream-weaver!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Advanced techniques I haven't mastered yet. Besides, pure intuition still is our most powerful tool in cases such as this! I simply had a feeling you were up for another bottle.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Way to make it sound like I'm some big experiment for you! Keep poking and prodding and eventually you'll get through my defenses and see the real me? Well this is it. And you know what? I don't want your Nectar anymore.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Including the one I just gave you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You said you didn't want my Nectar anymore but said nothing of Ambrosia, Eris, so... what do you say we visit the taverna once again?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I might visit the taverna, thanks! Though this time I'll be doing it alone. Don't take this the wrong way, but... I don't think we should be seen together anymore.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What, because you're concerned some baseless rumors will begin to spread? I thought Strife Incarnate wasn't supposed to care what anybody thought.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: It's for your own good. You're the one who's got a reputation to uphold. Really doesn't take much for me to live up to mine. I can just tell you to get out of my face!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You sure you won't come with me to the taverna anymore? Here's some Ambrosia for you either way. You'll know from its flavor that my intentions are true.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah, well! Not if I never try it! What if I just smash the bottle on the ground right here? What would you say to that?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd probably just offer you another. You get on my nerves all the time; Nemesis herself would say it's only fair that I occasionally get on yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Occasionally? Babe, you've been on my nerves for basically the whole time I've been back. I'm not falling for your tricks!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: For your stockpile of Ambrosia, Strife. Whether you choose to partake of it at the taverna with me, or someone else, or in a quiet moment on your own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You should know better than to mess with me like this. You know exactly what I am. I happen to kind of like you, but... I can make your life so miserable, you don't even know.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps it's your own influence on me but I'm prepared to take my chances. Why else would I keep speaking to you here? As part of some elaborate jest?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't know why, but the nicer you act with me, the stupider I think you are, and the more suspicious I get! And you've been acting real nice. This whole taverna thing, would you just... ask me normally next time?!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Can I get that Ambrosia back at least?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You know I can't accept this, now. Not after everything that's happened between us. Let's just... move on with our lives?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Huh? And, what exactly happened between us, would you say, if you had to summarize...? You're just... mocking me as usual, aren't you...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You're a long way from the Crossroads, Trouble! So, this is where the real action is, huh? Weren't you supposed to still be practicing?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Things took a turn, Eris...! You know how it goes. Now, if you'll excuse me, I have some urgent business to attend to.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Always in such a rush! I'd really hate to slow you down, though let's hang out sometime, OK? Have a great night.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Here to set another curse on me, Eris? For all your idiosyncrasies, I never took you for a traitor.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm not, I swear! It's just... ugh, you'll never understand. I wanted this to be a special moment, but you had to go and ruin it!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You again. What, are you concerned my task just isn't difficult enough? Going a bit too swimmingly?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Actually yes, I was a little worried about that. You got past the Sirens! What next, you'll go kill Chronos, and then what? Come on, babe. Let's not do anything rash.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Out of my way, Eris! I won't be wasting any further breath on you while you perpetuate this nonsense.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You never understood me, Trouble. Fine! You don't have to. Just remember that I'm doing this for your own good. And actually, mine too!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: What's all the commotion, anyway, huh? Chronos started bashing on Olympus again, sure. But it's not like you have to go after him now, do you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Believe me, I'd much rather stay and chitchat with you than sully my hands in Titan blood. But you have a choice, Eris. You can help me finish this, rather than hinder me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, babe... when you're right, you're right. You've given me a lot to chew on. Now you chew on this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What are you doing here in Tartarus? There's no way you could have access to this place, unless...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Unless I could, like, fly? Come off it, babe, you're way too stuck in your own head. You'll probably be stuck here too!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't take this personally, babe, but some things we just have to do, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: While I'd love to slow you down with a delightful chat, I think this will get the job done even better.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, heh! I know it's been a little rocky between us, but... we'll probably look back at this and laugh!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You don't look so happy to see me, babe! And I haven't even done anything yet...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Ungh! This isn't working...! Why won't you stop? What's it going to take, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You just don't know when to quit, do you, Trouble? Fine!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Something's up with you lately, babe! But you're not gonna tell me, so I'm just gonna start guessing. Is it Hecate? I bet it is, because something's up with her lately, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's nothing, it's just... I've not had opportunity to speak with Headmistress at length since all of this drew to a close. We had... a lot we needed to sort out. And still do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Aw, too bad! Even with all those scary issues you've been dealing with now mostly gone, turns out you've both got plenty of issues left. It's like they never go away!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: At least you don't have issues, Eris.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Moros was all giddy recently, at least for him. So you finally got a hold of his sisters, huh? You should feel lucky! They're my sisters too and we've never even met.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I saw a bit of you in them, perhaps, Eris. If their past work is any indication, they seem to value spontaneity... making things up as they go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: So bizarre, right? I mean why be like that when you could try and meticulously plan for every little detail in advance?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They're weavers... they do have to plan. That and spontaneity are not inherently opposed. Speaking of which, you must have heard they're planning an extended break.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Meaning we get to make things up as we go, for a change!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: We'll see how that turns out...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, um, babe? I kind of got you something... didn't want to make it a big deal or anything, so... here! For always being sweet.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A Nectar bottle... thank you, Eris. Though, we're breaking from tradition here. What if we just kept giving each other this same bottle back and forth, what then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Say it isn't so, Trouble! The Chronos stuff I'm hearing? He's really calling it quits? And he let Nyx and everybody off? Wh-what did you do to him?!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Working with my brother in a bygone time, we flooded our grandfather's mind with a cascade of new memories of the family he then realized he always had, creating in him a profound and sudden change. Why, what's your concern?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: My concern is that you ruined everything. So now we'll all have peace and harmony, joyful reunions, apologies, promises to never let this type of thing happen again?! Ungh!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Have you met our families, Eris? It's never that simple.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
endLines.text: It better not be, or I'll never forgive you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you even doing in this dump still, anyway? If your mom and pop are back in charge, you ought to be living in style with them down in Tartarus. Are they that bad?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No! I may have been born in Tartarus, but my home is here, as well as my responsibilities. And the Crossroads are not a dump, or at least wouldn't be if you could mind your rubbish.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Whatever! Though at least that's one good thing that came out of all this. I really thought I was gonna lose you, babe.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: How come you have to keep on doing all of this? I thought you won! But, you're still fighting! We're still fighting! I keep telling myself this is way too good to be true...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Suffice it to say we have some lingering matters to resolve, which require continuing the process that got us here. So congratulations! Things are going to stay messy for a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You don't mean that! You're just trying to get my hopes up! Well, don't you worry. Whatever it is you're planning, sooner or later I'll get involved.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I've learned to count on it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So was it worth it, Eris? Impeding me, making my task more difficult at every turn? You didn't prevent this outcome... you could have just aided me like so many others.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: And why would I have wanted to do that? Although I am a little disappointed in myself. Looking back, I could have caused so much more mayhem along the way...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well look at you, reflecting on how you could improve! Even if it's at making things worse for everybody else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You taught me that yourself, you know, Trouble? Not that I ever really learn, but... if I don't stay on top of things, maybe sometime in the future, everything will turn out fine...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're always in this corner on your own. Come to the taverna for a bit! We can let loose and have a good time, as you say. Got a bottle of Ambrosia right here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Babe that's so spontaneous of you! How could I say no? As long as I'm still allowed in there after what happened...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh! That stuff does go down smooth! How 'bout another? And nice job with this place! Really looks like you all fixed everything up!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This entire area was off limits for some time. It took a rather complex purification ritual and the efforts of a great many Shades to restore it, so... well, I won't even ask.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Good, I'm glad you won't even ask! Means we're really starting to get to know each other. Though, aren't you worried what the Shades are gonna say? I mean, about us...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, I hadn't considered that there might be some regrettable way to misconstrue our meeting; although in retrospect that was foolish. What do you expect they'll say?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you know! They see the way you talk to me, pick up after me, take me to the hot springs, give me Nectar, even Ambrosia. I mean, heh, come on! They're not stupid!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What? Eris, none of those things... an outing at the taverna is not some sort of... ungh. This simply was an opportunity to speak at greater length for once.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Hate to disappoint, but I didn't come here to speak. Now pour up! I don't have all night and this Ambrosia's way too bitter just for taking little swigs.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: ...That was so sweet of you to bring me along! And not a single piece of property destroyed! Glad we could do this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Eris? Care to join me over at the taverna for a quick break? If you're not otherwise preoccupied.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait babe are you asking me out? After all we've been through together, you're still trying to make a fresh start.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Last time you suggested I ask normally, so I attempted to. Now are we going for a blasted drink or not?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure! I'm... into it if you are, though... I don't see the point.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Whew! Now pour another one, come on. Both got a busy night ahead, so let's move it along!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All right, it's just... Ambrosia is meant more for sipping and savoring than gulping down in one incredibly long tilt.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: What makes you think I care? I won't feel anything if I take little tiny sips. Hardly feel anything right now!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you simply haven't given it a shot the proper way. You lift it to your lips like so. Briefly inhale, consider the aroma, and then drink. But only a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Oh. Ohhh. That's different, isn't it. I... didn't even know that's how it worked. Why are you being nice to me?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not entirely certain. Just to see what happens I suppose? You said before that you happen to kind of like me. I kind of like you, too! Even though I know I shouldn't...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, I feel sorry for you, babe. Really do. We always want what we can never have. Now show me how you sip this stuff again! Aren't you supposed to say something first?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Look that was fun and all but next I spot you at the Rift of Thessaly, I'm still gonna hit you with everything I've got! And you better keep showing up.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I'm planning on it. Don't ever change, Eris.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What if we head to the taverna for a little while? You don't seem particularly busy and we probably should talk...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: What if you get out of my face for a little while? Don't feel like seeing you and all those Shades judging me, thanks.

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: After everything that's happened I could definitely use a drink. Let's go right now!

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: We haven't been to the taverna in a bit, I guess, so I don't see why not. After you, babe!

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: Know what, I could definitely go for some Ambrosia here now that you bring it up!

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: ...You see those Shades right over there? I keep shooting dirty looks at them. I think they're too afraid to move!

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: ...This stuff really does taste kind of different every time. So what do we drink to? Don't even answer that, pour up.

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: ...You don't know how bad I want to flip all of these tables, so why don't we hurry up and drink.

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: Whew! I think I really needed that! But now I'm kind of sad.

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: Ah, it's funny. I don't hate that place as much as I used to.

options.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
options.text: Well, they won't be seeing me back there anytime soon!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: Thanks for the suggestion, ma'am, but I'll be sticking around as long as I want. Last I checked, you can't do anything about that, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You are correct, Eris, I cannot stop you from going where you please. But, I can make your life profoundly difficult, in ways you cannot possibly begin to imagine... I refrain only for the vow I made to your mother.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: You can't intimidate me. Nyx tried before and look where that got her!
